name: Regional Bouguer Low — Sedimentary Basin
description: Broad regional Bouguer low consistent with thick low-density sedimentary fill.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Regional Bouguer low is consistent with a {{entity}} — low-density sedimentary fill (ρ ≈ 2.2–2.5 g/cm³) produces negative anomaly relative to surrounding crystalline basement.
additionalRecommendations: Regional-residual separation required before local feature interpretation
Forward model to estimate basin thickness

name: Residual Bouguer High — Dense Body
description: Residual gravity high after regional removal indicates local dense body — mafic intrusion or basement high.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Residual gravity high is consistent with a locally dense body. Candidate interpretations include a {{entity}}, greenstone belt fragment, or basement high (ρ > 2.8 g/cm³).
additionalRecommendations: Regional-residual separation critical — confirm residual is not regional signal
Coincident magnetic data required to distinguish mafic from felsic high-density rocks

name: Residual Gravity Low — Felsic Intrusion
description: Residual gravity low consistent with felsic (granitic) intrusion.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Coincident residual gravity and magnetic lows are consistent with a {{entity}} — low density (ρ ≈ 2.6 g/cm³) and low susceptibility signature.
additionalRecommendations: Euler deconvolution recommended to estimate depth to source
Consider isostatic correction to remove crustal compensation effects

name: Terrain Correction Required
description: Gravity data in rugged terrain requires robust terrain correction — incomplete correction creates artefacts.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Processing assumption: Bouguer and terrain corrections must be verified before geological interpretation. Incomplete terrain correction in rugged topography can produce artefacts of ±5–20 mGal — larger than many geological signals.
additionalRecommendations: Confirm DEM resolution matches survey grid spacing
Apply complete Bouguer correction including terrain correction to at least 167 km radius

name: Regional-Residual Separation Required
description: Without regional-residual separation, local anomalies cannot be isolated from crustal/lithospheric signals.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Processing assumption: regional-residual separation is required before interpreting local gravity anomalies. The choice of separation wavelength is subjective and significantly affects residual anomaly shape and amplitude.
additionalRecommendations: Apply upward continuation at multiple heights to define regional field
Polynomial surface fitting is an alternative — document method in report

name: Density Contrast Uncertainty
description: Without density measurements, all gravity interpretations carry inherent density ambiguity.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Uncertainty warning: without direct density measurements (core samples, wireline logs), density contrasts are estimated from literature values. Uncertainty in density contrast of ±0.1 g/cm³ propagates to depth estimate uncertainty of ±20–30%.
additionalRecommendations: Sample nearby outcrop for bulk density measurements
Well log density data dramatically reduces this uncertainty
